Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated NT-proBNP, suggesting possible cardiac dysfunction or heart failure. There is mild microcytic anemia (low hematocrit, low iron, low HDL, high LDL), which may indicate iron deficiency and increased cardiovascular risk. Liver and kidney function, glucose, and electrolytes are within normal limits. Recommend further cardiac evaluation and assessment for iron deficiency and dyslipidemia management.
Provider Organization: St Matthews Health & Community Centre
